steps.title: Identify the noun phrase structure
steps.content: The phrase reads: “a photograph by British ______ Chris Killip.” The blank falls between “artist” (the expected word) and the proper name “Chris Killip,” testing whether they need punctuation between them.

steps.title: Apply the naming rule
steps.content:
When a proper name immediately identifies someone described by preceding nouns, no punctuation separates them—they form one identifying unit. “British artist Chris Killip” naturally combines nationality, profession, and name to identify the photographer. Choice C provides the unpunctuated “artist” that preserves this unified identification. The other options introduce unnecessary punctuation that fragments the unified phrase.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
